king, or a minister, be famous and will be endowed with many people.
42-45. THE MOON-SATURN CONJUNCTION IN ANGLES.
If the Moon and Saturn join in the Ascendant, the native will be in servitude, be base, given to
anger, be miserly, sleepy, lazy and sinful.
If the Moon and Saturn join in the 4th, one will earn through water, pearls and boats, be engaged
in excavation (or in mines etc.), be excellent and agreeable to others.
If the Moon and Saturn join in the 7th, the subject will be devoid of wife, be supreme among his
townsmen and be honoured by the king.
If the Moon and Saturn join in the 10th, the person concerned will win the enemies with his Army
of horses, be famous, is a bad womans son and will be head of men.
46. DISPOSITION OF THE MOON.
If the Moon is conjunct benefics she will always prove beneficial.
If the Moon joins Mars/Saturn, she is always inauspicious; this does not apply to her joining
